def: A gas washer. See under Gas.
Of or pertaining to peat, or turf; of the nature of peat, orturf; peaty; turfy. Sir. W. Scott.
One of the spaces between the septa in the Anthozoa.
A fluosilicate; a salt of silicofluoric acid.
A fruit consisting of many dry indehiscent cells, which containbut few seeds and cohere about a common style, as in the mallows.

A kind of plain sleigh drawn by one horse; originally, a rudeoblong box on runners. [U.S.]Sledges or pungs, coarsely framed of split saplings, and surmountedwith a large crockery crate. Judd.They did not take out the pungs to-day. E. E. Hale.

Any plant of the genus Drosera, low bog plants whose leaves arebeset with pediceled glands which secrete a viscid fluid thatglitters like dewdrops and attracts and detains insects. After aninsect is caught, the glands curve inward like tentacles and the leafdigests it. Called also lustwort.
Resembling a goose; silly; simple. Sydney Smith.
